GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Everything you need to know about modern Australian craftsmanship", "Oroton's creative director, Sophie Holt, shares how she reinterpreted the rich heritage of the iconic label.", "Since its inception in 1938, Oroton has evolved from an established importer of luxury European textiles to a fashion house in its own right, and in turn, cemented itself as a leading Australian label. Despite this evolution, the label has not once wavered from its original commitment to craftsmanship and innovative design. Intent on sourcing the very best materials from around the world, Oroton has become known for its uniquely Australian, yet rather modern, take on leather goods, accessories and ready-to-wear pieces. With a focus on quality and craftsmanship still at the forefront of each of the brand's designs, creative director Sophie Holt has steadfastly breathed new life into the label since her appointment in 2018 with her fresh interpretation on its modern classics. "Since I joined the iconic brand, I have really focused on reinvigorating and re-interpreting Oroton's rich heritage by injecting a fresh spirit for now, evolving the leather goods product offering and launching full ready-to-wear collections," she tells Vogue. The celebrated woman at the helm of the label adds that, as Oroton continues to evolve, she plans to carry on "creating easy-to-wear pieces that are unfussy and contemporary, [and] designed to elevate everyday dressing. Utility, style and quality underpin everything we stand for in our world of real life luxury," adding that "luxurious leather goods and craftsmanship of the highest quality are cornerstones of the brand." From the various suppliers Oroton chooses to work with, to the fabrics the brand sources—which Holt shares come from "some of the most respected mills in Italy and Japan"—every aspect of the fashion house's production process aligns with the creative director's aim to put quality before quantity. "Established in 1938, [Oroton] is one of Australia's oldest design houses synonymous with everyday luxury. Today, everything we do centres around quality and craftsmanship," she says. Holt also confirms that the starting point for each of the label's collections begins with a feeling or a mood. From there, she works with two different design teams who each help her narrow down the collection's fabrics, colours and key silhouettes. "The customer looks to us for fashionable yet timeless products that can be worn season after season," she explains. "The quality of our craftsmanship and raw materials ensures that our products stand the test of time and can be worn over generations." This season, Holt was inspired by the idea of a vintage traveller, which feels timely given our current inability to move freely. "We drew from vintage maps, sun faded stripes and seashell prints. We worked with washed back colours, soft silks and leathers, and Italian linens to bring the collection to life," she tells Vogue of the label's latest offering, which incorporates luxurious fabrics and a vibrant colour palette with Oroton's time honoured techniques. "Wearing Oroton gives you a sense of confidence and style that looks effortlessly pulled together," adds Holt, as though the homegrown label's impressive focus on quality and craftsmanship weren't convincing enough to start adding its products to your cart. "We hope you enjoy having Oroton as part of your life as much as we do."
